title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: need install AutoSpectral, run bit first: significantly faster processing, also recommend installing companion C++ acceleration package. optional meaningful effect runtime, particularly automated spectral extraction per-cell autofluorescence steps. Windows, install Rtools first. installed, AutoSpectralRcpp used automatically wherever helps — action needed.
code: # Install Bioconductor packages if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))   install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install(c("flowWorkspace", "FlowSOM"))  # You'll need devtools or remotes to install from GitHub. install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("DrCytometer/AutoSpectral") remotes::install_github("DrCytometer/AutoSpectralRcpp")

title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: Download data today’s example Mendeley Data. data 7-colour panel run 5-laser Cytek Aurora. samples spleen, lung liver. pretty simple experiment, nice since run quickly. different tissues allow us look handle diverse autofluorescence profiles. ’ll point panel deliberately designed accommodate autofluorescence peaks, can expect autofluorescence removal work pretty well method.

text: Now can load AutoSpectral.
code: library(AutoSpectral)

title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: Since default cytometer Aurora, can actually just call without arguments. Otherwise need specify cytometer ’re using. controls? must typed correctly. Create control file. need manually edit control file, telling AutoSpectral ’s going . try fill stuff , check . See article GitHub Colibri Cytometry. get warnings ’ve got bead cell controls, AutoSpectral like pick one per fluorophore. ’s better one control per fluorophore ’ll use spectral profiles matrix unmixing, two instances thing, create big mess. ’s control file looks like first generated: Fill verify marker, fluorophore, control.type, universal.negative columns appropriate. detail, see Control File article. automated workflow need fill gate.name gate.define. columns used legacy gating pipeline. channel column peak emission channel (one receives lot signal) also optional automated pipeline. peak channel automatically determined data. ’s completed control file look like workflow: ’ve got way want, write name control file run error checking function. either tell didn’t find issues, , likely, provide table potential issues consider fixing. See help using ?check.control.file.
code: asp <- get.autospectral.param(   cytometer = "aurora",   figures = TRUE # plot figures throughout to show what's going on ) control.dir <- "./Raw/Set1/Reference Group" create.control.file(control.dir, asp) control.file <- "fcs_control_file.csv" check.control.file(control.dir, control.file, asp)

previous_headings: Getting your fluorophore spectra from the controls
title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: automated workflow replaces three-step legacy pipeline (define.flow.control → clean.controls → get.fluorophore.spectra) single call: hood, get.spectra.automated() performs several -place cleaning steps automatically, without requiring define tune gates: Saturation removal — detector-saturated events excluded per-fluorophore processing begins. Singlet gating — doublets automatically excluded. AF orthogonalisation — single-stained control, autofluorescence vector (paired universal.negative specified control file, estimated internally lower quartile control ) projected identify events whose signal genuinely driven fluorophore rather background. Cosine-similarity filtering — top-expressing candidate events ranked cosine similarity AF vector. Events AF-like excluded, retaining fluorophore-rich subset. KNN scatter-matched AF subtraction — retained event, nearest-neighbour unstained events scatter space averaged subtracted, giving per-event background-corrected spectral signature. Automated QC legacy pipeline fallback — resulting spectrum compared spectral reference library (via cosine similarity). similarity default threshold, function automatically re-runs particular control using original AutoSpectral pipeline control cleaning robust linear modelling along expected peak channel rather reporting failure. legacy approach produces spectrum like reference spectrum, one kept. event fallback check, versions spectrum saved inspect. default settings work well across wide range panels, key parameters can adjusted needed:
code: spectra <- get.spectra.automated(   control.dir     = control.dir,   control.def.file = control.file,   asp             = asp ) spectra <- get.spectra.automated(   control.dir           = control.dir,   control.def.file      = control.file,   asp                   = asp,   n.candidates          = 1000L,   # candidate events selected per fluorophore   n.spectral            = 200L,    # events retained after cosine filtering   k.neighbors           = 2L,      # scatter neighbours for AF subtraction   cosine.threshold      = 0.9,     # min cosine vs reference library   peak.signal.threshold = 0.5      # min normalised signal in expected peak channel )

title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: figures = TRUE (default), get.spectra.automated() produces several diagnostic outputs: Spectral traces heatmap (figure_spectra/) — standard outputs legacy pipeline. Check expected profiles cytometer online viewers Cytek Cloud QC plots. Spectral Signature Traces Spectral Signature Heatmap Cosine similarity heatmap (figure_similarity_heatmap/) — shows pairwise similarity fluorophore spectra. Cosine Similarity Heatmap Hotspot matrix (figure_similarity_heatmap/) — highlights fluorophore pairs high spectral overlap, Mage et al. Values 6 likely cause unmixing problems; values 4 6 worth reviewing. Hotspot Matrix Heatmap Per-fluorophore cosine-filter traces (figure_spectra/) — multi-panel PDF showing, control, events distributed cosine similarity AF vector. Quantiles coloured least (blue) (red) AF-like. quick, automated approach intrusive AF noise exclusion takes place gating-based exclusion AutoSpectral <1.6.0. ’ll notice brightest events typically cleanest, expected (farthest background). Hotspot Matrix Heatmap Scatter-match plots (figure_scatter_match/) — multi-panel PDF showing, fluorophore, unstained background population, selected spectral events, matched AF/negative events used subtraction. now done using k-nearest neighbours, essentially perfect. ’s CD11b - BUV805: Hotspot Matrix Heatmap Spectral QC report (figure_spectral_ribbon/) — per-fluorophore PDF comparing extracted spectrum reference library entry instrument. Fluorophores triggered RLM fallback flagged, automated refined spectra shown applicable. Spectral QC Report Spectral QC BUV395 Spectral QC BUV395 Spectra CSV — final normalised spectra matrix saved table_spectra/. can reload later using read.spectra() pass directly unmixing function. Call list.files("table_spectra/") find files folder. default, new spectral profile extraction pipeline include autofluorescence parameter output spectra. try reduce confusion. want single AF, might SpectroFlo software, can set return.af=TRUE call get.spectra.automated(). extract mean AF background unstained sample label “AF” fluorophore column control file. Note ID7000 users: ID7000 can set basically voltage settings controls, spectral signatures really consistent machines (experience). , spectral references AutoSpectral informative. order avoid “mismatches” trigger legacy refinement, thresholds relaxed ID7000 files. fine, since new automated pipeline tends least accurate legacy approach.

title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: Tips: ’re getting lot LEGACY_REFINEMENT flags, check peak.signal.threshold cosine.threshold together QC log assuming controls bad. genuinely dim control show low PeakSignal well low CosineSim. n.candidates n.spectral matter external-negative mode (.e. universal.negative file paired control). internal-negative mode, top-5%/bottom-10% event split fixed two parameters silently ignored. function always writes two CSVs table_spectra/: Automated_<name>.csv (best-choice, may include legacy-adopted spectra) Automated_original_<name>.csv (always -automated). ’re benchmarking automated pipeline , use _original_ file legacy fallbacks don’t skew comparison.

title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: AutoSpectral provides options unmixing. Let’s start basic, replicating OLS unmixing SpectroFlo (Aurora data). default, automated spectral extraction pipeline provide autofluorescence signature, comparable performing unmixing without autofluorescence extraction. Autofluorescence best handled via per-cell approach detailed . need minimal flow.control object feeding dye marker names unmixing. automated workflow, created using reload.flow.control() tool: unmix, specify file (path) FCS file want unmix: Note just using OLS–“AutoSpectral” unmixing method, just using AutoSpectral R package perform bog standard unmixing. folder full FCS files, can files folder. Note essentially just lapply loop files. can, however, parallelized (set parallel=TRUE). Memory usage handled via file chunking, can modify using chunk.size argument, needed. default, unmixed files generated Autospectral_unmixed, can change passing path output.dir. want use weighted least-squares, call like : details WLS unmixing, including calculating re-using weights detailed dedicated article Okay, ’s basic unmixing. , think see bit improvement using AutoSpectral even unmixing algorithms due improvements single-colour control handling. .
code: flow.control <- reload.flow.control(   control.dir      = control.dir,   control.def.file = control.file,   asp              = asp ) spleen.fcs.file <- "./Raw/Set1/Stained/D4 Spleen_Set1.fcs" unmix.fcs(   spleen.fcs.file,   spectra,   asp,   flow.control,   method = "OLS",   file.suffix = "without AF extraction" ) unmix.folder(   fcs.dir = "./Raw/Set1/Stained/",   spectra = spectra,   asp = asp,   flow.control = flow.control,   method = "OLS" # use OLS unmixing (not AutoSpectral unmixing) ) unmix.fcs(spleen.fcs.file, spectra, asp, flow.control, method = "WLS")

title: 01 Automated Spectral Workflow
text: per-cell autofluorescence extraction per-cell fluorophore optimization, AutoSpectral needs information. extract autofluorescence signatures three tissues involved , look use unmixing. ’ll also get information fluorophore emission variability use try improve unmixing. go use information unmixing, select method = AutoSpectral. Per-cell autofluorescence extraction substantially faster AutoSpectralRcpp installed (see Installation section ). installed, takes automatically — action needed. use per-cell autofluorescence extraction , fluorophore optimization, : Using refine=TRUE take longer, get.af.spectra() call subsequent unmixing calls. benefit primarily messier samples, particularly tissues. just using PBMCs nice lymphoid samples like spleen, probably won’t see much benefit . get distribution autofluorescence spectra spectral trace heatmap figure_autofluorescence. AF spectra saved CSV file table_spectra. Autofluorescence profiles spleen can also look distribution autofluorescence like , black line represents median signature (might use automated single AF parameter), red traces represents variation: Autofluorescence variation spleen also get images showing us impact AF extraction unstained sample supplied. without AF extraction, looking AutoSpectral determined two -affected fluorophore channels: Spleen: AF Extraction get unstained sample per-cell AF extraction, without refinement (refine=FALSE): Spleen: Per-Cell AF Extraction get unstained sample per-cell AF extraction, refinement (refine=TRUE): Spleen: Per-Cell AF Extraction Refined want samples containing different autofluorescence profiles, , extract AF spectral variation type unstained sample. provide corresponding af.spectra unmixing call. unmixing call can single FCS file, can , , folder. , whole set stained lung samples, ’d pull AF spectra unstained lung sample, call unmix.folder folder containing lung (lung) samples. Repeat type autofluorescence sample. Read per-cell autofluorescence extraction works GitHub Colibri article. case, three types samples: spleen, liver lung tissues. working human PBMCs, usually single (optionally pooled) unstained PBMC sample fine. , however, samples sick donors, might consider collecting unstained sample donor matching autofluorescence closely. easier set Honeychrome app, can quickly assign inspect results visual interface. can easily set loop lapply loop matching elements names list. detail per-cell AF extraction, see dedicated article subject.
code: spleen.unstained <- "./Raw/Set1/Unstained/D1 Spleen_Set1.fcs" spleen.af <- get.af.spectra(   unstained.sample = spleen.unstained,   asp = asp,   spectra = spectra,   refine = TRUE # optional; when TRUE, more AF spectra will be generated, focusing on problem cells. This takes longer, though. ) unmix.fcs(   fcs.file = spleen.fcs.file,   spectra = spectra,   asp = asp,   flow.control = flow.control,   method = "AutoSpectral", # use AutoSpectral unmixing   af.spectra = spleen.af, # use these AF signatures as the options   file.suffix = "per-cell AF extraction" ) lung.unstained <- "./Raw/Set1/Unstained/D2 Lung_Set1.fcs" lung.af <- get.af.spectra(lung.unstained, asp, spectra) # use refine=TRUE for a modest improvement, default is FALSE lung.fcs.file <- "./Raw/Set1/Stained/D5 Lung_Set1.fcs" unmix.fcs(   lung.fcs.file,   spectra,   asp,   flow.control,   method = "AutoSpectral",   af.spectra = lung.af,   file.suffix = "per-cell AF extraction" )  liver.unstained <- "./Raw/Set1/Unstained/D3 Liver_Set1.fcs" liver.af <- get.af.spectra(liver.unstained, asp, spectra) # use refine=TRUE for a modest improvement, default is FALSE liver.fcs.file <- "./Raw/Set1/Stained/D6 Liver_Set1.fcs" unmix.fcs(   liver.fcs.file,   spectra,   asp,   flow.control,   method = "AutoSpectral",   af.spectra = liver.af,   file.suffix = "per-cell AF extraction" )

title: 04 ID7000 WLS Example
text: basic example non-AutoSpectral unmixing workflow, using samples ID7000. illustrates weighted least squares unmixing. per-cell autofluorescence extraction per-cell fluorophore optimization, see articles topics. point workflow show control clean-automated determination fluorophore spectra can mimic improve upon unmixing obtained Sony using WLSM unmixing. workflow just uses weighted least squares (WLS) unmixing. Please see Full Workflow NOTE yet updated reflect changes version 1.5.0. See full workflow article detail. still work , control process now available.
code: library( AutoSpectral )  # Define the location of the single-stained control files. control.dir <- "./Cell controls"  # Get the parameters for your cytometer. # Supported cytometers include "aurora", "id7000", "a8", "s8" and "opteon". asp <- get.autospectral.param( cytometer = "id7000", figures = TRUE )  # Optionally, create a control file (see article on this). # After creating it, manually edit to ensure it is correct. create.control.file( control.dir, asp )  # Locate the edited control file. control.def.file <- "fcs_control_file.csv"  # check the control file for errors control.file.errors <- check.control.file( control.dir, control.def.file, asp )  ## Adjustments to automated gating # This will be covered more extensively elsewhere. # in this case, the cells on scatter are very small, so we modify the target. asp$gate.bound.density.max.target.cells <- 0  # Load the control files, gate, prepare for spectral extraction. # This is usually the slowest step. flow.control <- define.flow.control( control.dir, control.def.file, asp )  # For speed and accuracy, the recommended approach is to clean the controls # using separate (universal) negative samples for both beads and cells. # In doing so, we select cells with matching autofluorescent background and # restrict the calculations to the brightest events. flow.control <- clean.controls( flow.control, asp )  # Extract the clean spectra univ.neg.spectra <- get.fluorophore.spectra(    flow.control,    asp,    use.clean.expr = TRUE,    title = "Universal Negative Cells"  )  # By default AutoSpectral extracts autofluorescence as a spectrum. # This is comparable to "Autofluorescence as a Fluorescent Tag" in SpectroFlo # More on this later. # To remove this AF parameter for unmixing: no.af.spectra <- univ.neg.spectra[ ! rownames( univ.neg.spectra ) == "AF", ]  ## Unmixing # Set the location of the raw files to be unmixed. sample.dir <- "./Raw samples"  # Perform unmixing, here we will unmix all fcs files in the folder using  # Weighted least squares (WLS or in Sony lingo, WLSM). unmix.folder(    sample.dir,    no.af.spectra,    asp,    flow.control,    method = "WLS"  )

what: Multipass AF assignment for complex samples
title: 10 Single Cell Autofluorescence
text: particularly messy samples (tissues, tumors), single AF subtraction pass per cell may remove everything , particularly higher-abundance “worst offender” cells. joint unmixing pipeline underlying unmix.fcs() (see unmix.autospectral.rcpp(), backed unmix_autospectral_joint_pipeline.cpp) supports second pass AF assignment, top whatever spectra generated get.af.spectra(). controlled two arguments unmix.fcs(): n.af.passes (default 1L): number AF assignment passes run per cell. Setting 2 re-scores residual left first pass AF library , better match found, adds AF contribution top first pass’s estimate. refine.af.quantile (default 0.5): controls cells taken forward additional pass(es). cells quantile first-pass AF abundance re-scored; rest left -. Set 1 force every cell additional passes, though rarely necessary increase unmixing time. Testing suggests value around 0.7 good problematic samples, 0.5 generous. get.af.spectra(refine = TRUE), aimed complex AF samples comes cost processing time proportional fraction cells re-scored. clean PBMC-type samples, default n.af.passes = 1 usually sufficient. unmix.fcs() also exposes several joint-pipeline tuning parameters (cell.weight, alpha, collinear.threshold, joint.pair.resolution, noise.floor) control per-cell fluorophore variant optimisation rather AF extraction; see ?unmix.fcs details.
code: unmix.fcs( fcs.file = file.path( fully.stained.dir, "C3 Lung_GFP_003_Samples.fcs" ),            spectra = spectra,            asp = asp,            flow.control = flow.control,            method = "AutoSpectral",            af.spectra = lung.af,            n.af.passes = 2L,            refine.af.quantile = 0.5 )

title: 11 Per Cell Optimization
text: arguments let tune joint pipeline beyond n.passes: Multipass autofluorescence refinement. n.af.passes (default 1) controls many AF extraction passes run per cell. first pass uses matching pursuit assign AF candidate every cell; n.af.passes greater 1, subsequent passes revisit highest-abundance AF cells (controlled refine.af.quantile, default 0.5, fraction cells taken forward reassessment — set 1 reassess everyone) can refine AF assignment . Like n.passes, trades additional computation time improved AF characterization, useful tissue samples heterogeneous autofluorescence; PBMC data typically shows negligible benefit. Collinear fluorophore handling. collinear.threshold (default 0.5) sets cosine similarity two fluorophore variants treated structurally collinear (e.g. APC/BUV661) routed conflict resolution rather independent assignment. Lower values trigger conflict resolution often (thorough, slower); higher values allow variants committed based minimizing spillover brighter fluorophore alone, faster can understate dimmer collinear fluorophore’s true abundance. joint.pair.resolution (default TRUE) turns conflict-resolution step ; enabled, small combinatorial test run specifically competing pair(s) find better joint solution. Per-cell detector weighting. cell.weight (default FALSE, TRUE automatically ID7000 data) applies per-cell weighting joint solve based detector’s fitted signal, noise.floor (default 125) preventing near-dark channels receiving runaway weight. alpha (default 0.5) balances residual minimization covariance-based spillover minimization scoring candidate variants. users won’t need touch — defaults chosen testing across Aurora, ID7000 S8 data — ’re ’re working unusually noisy panel want explore impact unmixing. Unmixed FCS files appear folder ./autospectral_unmixed. Add file.suffix argument want tag FCS file’s name differentiate versions.

title: 11 Per Cell Optimization
text: Setting “speed” determines many pre-screened candidate variants tested per cell. options “fast” = 1, “medium” = 3, “slow” = 10. based quite bit testing high parameter data Aurora, ID7000 S8. data messier fluorophores (like BUV661 ), using “slow” likely show improvement. using modern fluorophores, may find minimal difference now using “fast”. want set number variants , can providing n.variants argument. override “speed”. call , instance, ’ve requested test 100 variants per cell (depending ’s actually available). comparable previous “slow” brute force approach.
code: unmix.fcs( fcs.file = file.path( fully.stained.dir, "C3 Lung_GFP_003_Samples.fcs" ),            spectra = spectra,            asp = asp,            flow.control = flow.control,            method = "AutoSpectral",            af.spectra = lung.af,            spectra.variants = variants,            n.variants = 100 )

Upgrading BLAS LAPACK (see ) also takes several minutes ’re taking time read instructions carefully. Run-time vary considerably depending size quantity files ’re processing. benchmark 42-color Aurora dataset using single-stained cell controls plenty data, slow steps pre-processing pipeline define.flow.control() clean.controls(). Aurora dataset aforementioned i7 Windows laptop, get: define.flow.control() v0.8.7: 12min sequential, 9min parallel define.flow.control() v0.9.0: 4min sequential, 2min parallel clean.controls() v0.8.7: 11min sequential, possible parallel clean.controls() v0.9.0: 11min sequential, 6.5min parallel, fully optimized get.spectral.variants() v.0.8.7: 2min sequential, 1min parallel get.spectral.variants() v.0.9.0: 65sec sequential, 32sec parallel run faster v1.0.0 datasets lots cells changed default plotting settings limit maximum number events plotted (plotting lots points slow R, even accelerated scattermore package). Unmixing time depends following variable: file size (number cells/events, including debris) number detectors (ID7000 data takes bit longer) number fluorophores unmixing algorithm OLS WLS fast, per-cell AF extraction slower, per-cell fluorophore optimization slower still. version 1.0.0, time required quite bit (see ). using AutoSpectralRcpp, per-cell methods likely run 10x slower. Benchmarks “C3 Lung_GFP_003_Samples.fcs”, 8-core laptop, using OpenBLAS AutoSpectralRcpp, applicable: unmix.fcs() WLS OLS v0.8.7: 9sec unmix.fcs() WLS OLS v0.9.0: 9sec unmix.fcs() WLS OLS v1.0.0: 9-10sec (handling FCS file) unmix.fcs() perCell AF extraction v0.8.7: 2min unmix.fcs() perCell AF extraction v0.9.0: 1min unmix.fcs() perCell AF extraction v1.0.0: 14sec (45s R without C++) unmix.fcs() perCell fluorophore optimization “fast” v0.8.7: 9min unmix.fcs() perCell fluorophore optimization “fast” v0.9.0: <2min unmix.fcs() perCell fluorophore optimization “fast” v1.0.0: <2min (note: performance now comparable previous “slow”) unmix.fcs() perCell fluorophore optimization “slow” v0.8.7: 62min unmix.fcs() perCell fluorophore optimization “slow” v0.9.0: 19min unmix.fcs() perCell fluorophore optimization “slow” v1.0.0: 11min unmix.folder() WLS OLS, 6 files, v0.8.7: 67sec sequential, interrupted parallel unmix.folder() WLS OLS, 6 files, v0.9.0: 62sec sequential, 31sec parallel expect major gains, although look GPU acceleration.

title: 13 ID7000 WLS Example
text: basic example workflow, using samples ID7000. illustrates weighted least squares unmixing. per-cell autofluorescence extraction per-cell fluorophore optimization, see articles topics. point workflow show control clean-automated determination fluorophore spectra can mimic improve upon unmixing obtained Sony using WLSM unmixing. workflow just uses weighted least squares (WLS) unmixing. Please see Full Workflow
code: library( AutoSpectral )  # Define the location of the single-stained control files. control.dir <- "./Cell controls"  # Get the parameters for your cytometer. # Supported cytometers include "aurora", "id7000", "a8", "s8" and "opteon". asp <- get.autospectral.param( cytometer = "id7000", figures = TRUE )  # Optionally, create a control file (see article on this). # After creating it, manually edit to ensure it is correct. create.control.file( control.dir, asp )  # Locate the edited control file. control.def.file <- "fcs_control_file.csv"  # check the control file for errors control.file.errors <- check.control.file( control.dir, control.def.file, asp )  ## Adjustments to automated gating # This will be covered more extensively elsewhere. # in this case, the cells on scatter are very small, so we modify the target. asp$gate.bound.density.max.target.cells <- 0  # Load the control files, gate, prepare for spectral extraction. # This is usually the slowest step. flow.control <- define.flow.control( control.dir, control.def.file, asp )  # For speed and accuracy, the recommended approach is to clean the controls # using separate (universal) negative samples for both beads and cells. # In doing so, we select cells with matching autofluorescent background and # restrict the calculations to the brightest events. flow.control <- clean.controls( flow.control, asp )  # Extract the clean spectra univ.neg.spectra <- get.fluorophore.spectra(    flow.control,    asp,    use.clean.expr = TRUE,    title = "Universal Negative Cells"  )  # By default AutoSpectral extracts autofluorescence as a spectrum. # This is comparable to "Autofluorescence as a Fluorescent Tag" in SpectroFlo # More on this later. # To remove this AF parameter for unmixing: no.af.spectra <- univ.neg.spectra[ ! rownames( univ.neg.spectra ) == "AF", ]  ## Unmixing # Set the location of the raw files to be unmixed. sample.dir <- "./Raw samples"  # Perform unmixing, here we will unmix all fcs files in the folder using  # Weighted least squares (WLS or in Sony lingo, WLSM). unmix.folder(    sample.dir,    no.af.spectra,    asp,    flow.control,    method = "WLS"  )

s8.fcs ) chorus.spectra[ 1:6, 1:5 ] spectral.heatmap( chorus.spectra, title = "OMIP102", color.palette = "mako",                   show.legend = FALSE ) spectral.trace( chorus.spectra, title = "OMIP102", split.lasers = FALSE,                 show.legend = FALSE ) spectral.trace( chorus.spectra, title = "OMIP102", split.lasers = FALSE,                 show.legend = TRUE ) spectral.trace( chorus.spectra, title = "OMIP102", split.lasers = TRUE ) cosine.similarity.plot( chorus.spectra, title = "Cosine_OMIP102",                          output.dir = getwd() ) matrix.subset <- chorus.spectra[ 1:5, ] small.cosine.matrix <- cosine.similarity( matrix.subset ) create.heatmap( small.cosine.matrix, number.labels = TRUE,                 color.palette = "turbo", plot.dir = getwd() ) create.heatmap( small.cosine.matrix,                  number.labels = TRUE,                 legend.label = "Cosine Similarity",                 triangular = TRUE,                 color.palette = create.biplot( omip102.data,                x.dim = "BUV395-A", y.dim = "BUV496-A",                asp,                title = "OMIP102_biplot1" ) create.biplot( omip102.data,                x.dim = "BUV395-A", y.dim = "BUV496-A",                x.min = -15000, x.max = 1e6,                color.palette = "inferno",                asp,                 title = "OMIP102_biplot2" ) create.biplot( omip102.data,                x.dim = "BUV395-A", y.dim = "BUV496-A",                x.min = -15000, x.max = 1e6,                y.width.basis = -250,                color.palette = "inferno",                asp,                  title = "OMIP102_biplot3" ) create.biplot( omip102.data,                x.dim = "BUV395-A", y.dim = "BUV496-A",                x.min = -15000, x.max = 1e6,                y.max = 2e6,                max.points = 1e8,                color.palette = "viridis",                asp,                   title = "OMIP102_biplot4" )

AF Benchmarking
code: # compare two different AF extractions (e.g. with and without refinement) af_v1 <- get.af.spectra( unstained.fcs, asp, spectra, refine = FALSE ) af_v2 <- get.af.spectra( unstained.fcs, asp, spectra, refine = TRUE )  cmp <- compare.af(   unstained.fcs   = unstained.fcs,   spectra         = spectra,   af.spectra.list = list( no_refine = af_v1, refined = af_v2 ),   assign.fn       = "assign.af.joint.cov",   n.downsample    = 2000,   plot.dir        = "figures/af_compare" )  # mean cosine similarity for each candidate sapply( cmp[ c("no_refine","refined") ], `[[`, "Mean_Sim" ) my.assign.af <- function( raw.data, spectra, af.spectra ) {   # raw.data   : matrix, cells x detectors   # spectra    : matrix, fluorophores x detectors (no AF row)   # af.spectra : matrix, AF variants x detectors    # ... your logic here ...    af.idx  # integer vector, length nrow(raw.data), values in 1:nrow(af.spectra) } fit.my.af <- function( raw.data, unmixed, unmixing.matrix, spectra, af.spectra ) {   # unmixed         : matrix, cells x fluorophores (OLS result without AF)   # unmixing.matrix : matrix, fluorophores x detectors    # ... your logic here ...    list(     unmixed = unmixed_with_af_corrected,  # cells x fluorophores, no AF column     af.idx  = af.idx                      # integer vector   ) } results <- test.af.accuracy(   unstained.fcs = "path/to/unstained.fcs",   spectra       = my.spectra,      # fluorophores x detectors   af.spectra    = my.af.spectra,   # AF variants x detectors   asp           = my.asp,   functions     = c(     "assign.af.fluorophores",  # existing method - keep as a reference point     "assign.af.residuals",     # existing method     "my.assign.af"             # your new function   ),   n.downsample  = 1000,        # events per run; increase for a final check   plot.dir      = "figures/af_dev" )  # mean cosine similarity for each method (higher is better) sapply( results, `[[`, "Mean_Sim" )  # per-cell similarities for your method hist( results[["my.assign.af"]]$Similarity, breaks = 50,       main = "Per-cell cosine similarity", xlab = "Cosine similarity" ) bench <- benchmark.af.spectra(   unstained.fcs = "path/to/unstained.fcs",   spectra       = my.spectra,   af.spectra    = my.af.spectra,   asp           = my.asp,   functions     = c(     "assign.af.fluorophores",     "assign.af.residuals",     "my.assign.af"   ),   n.fluors     = c( 5, 10, 20, 30, 40 ),  # panel sizes to test   n.draws      = 5,                        # random draws per panel size   n.downsample = 1000,   plot.dir     = "figures/af_dev" )  # the summary data frame: mean and SD of cosine similarity across draws head( bench$summary )  # the ggplot object, if you want to tweak the figure bench$plot + ggplot2::ggtitle( "My new AF method" )

code: asp      <- get.autospectral.param("Aurora") spectra  <- get.fluorophore.spectra(panel, asp) unstained.fcs <- "./path/to/my.fcs" af.spectra  <- get.af.spectra(unstained.fcs, asp, spectra)  sim <- sim.flow.data(   spectra    = spectra,   asp        = asp,   n.cells    = 20000,   af.spectra = af.spectra )  # Ground-truth vs assigned AF abundance plot(sim$truth$af.abundance, sim$unmixed.no.af[, "AF"])  create.biplot(   sim$unmixed.no.af,   "APC", "BUV661", # your fluorophores   x.min = -10000, # adjust axes if needed   asp,   save = FALSE )

title: 18 Weighted Least Squares Unmixing
text: Ordinary least squares (OLS) unmixing treats every detector equally reliable. practice, detectors equally reliable. Poisson noise model, absolute noise scales signal level: channel carrying high photon counts high absolute variance therefore less precise measurement. Weighted least squares (WLS) unmixing accounts -weighting noisier high-signal channels -weighting quieter low-signal channels, measurements precise.

title: 18 Weighted Least Squares Unmixing
text: Flow cytometry data approximate Poisson process: number photons detected given time window Poisson distributed. central property Poisson distribution variance equals mean. means channel higher average signal also higher variance, therefore higher absolute noise. standard WLS weight channel \(j\) therefore: \[w_j = \frac{1}{\text{mean signal channel } j}\] High mean signal means high absolute variance therefore low weight. Low mean signal means low absolute variance: channel reliably reads near zero, solver treats reading precise. Note opposite coefficient variation (CV) suggest – dim channel large CV small absolute noise. WLS, least AutoSpectral, weights absolute Poisson noise, relative noise. lower bound 1e-6 applied taking reciprocal avoid division--zero channels record essentially nothing. practice, WLS particularly recommended cytometers Sony ID7000, FACSDiscover A8, FACSDiscover S8, large numbers detectors therefore -determined.

what: calculate.weights() – extracting weights from a file
title: 18 Weighted Least Squares Unmixing
text: calculate.weights() reads FCS file, extracts spectral detector columns, computes inverse-mean weights described . return value named numeric vector, one element per spectral detector. Names match detector channel names FCS file can matched unambiguously spectra matrix later. can optionally save weights CSV inspection later reuse: saved CSV can read back utils::read.csv() weights column extracted named vector.
code: library( AutoSpectral )  # weights for a single representative file weights <- calculate.weights(   fcs.file        = "./samples/representative_sample.fcs",   spectral.channels = flow.control$spectral.channel ) weights <- calculate.weights(   fcs.file          = "./samples/representative_sample.fcs",   spectral.channels = flow.control$spectral.channel,   save              = TRUE,   output.dir        = "./table_spectra",   filename          = "weights.csv" )

title: 18 Weighted Least Squares Unmixing
text: unmix.wls() takes matrix raw spectral measurements, spectra matrix, optional weight vector, returns unmixed fluorophore abundances. weights NULL, unmix.wls() falls back computing inverse-column-mean weights internally raw.data. convenient one-calls means weights derived whatever data pass moment. consistent results across multiple files, compute reuse shared weight vector (see ). Internally, unmix.wls() constructs diagonal weight matrix \(W\), applies spectra matrix \(\) form \(^* = W^{1/2} \), solves via SVD numerical stability: \[\hat{x} = (^T W )^{-1} ^T W \, y\] SVD path preferred direct matrix inversion weighting can make system poorly conditioned channels low signal.
code: # raw.data: cells x detectors # spectra:  fluorophores x detectors (normalised 0-1) unmixed <- unmix.wls( raw.data, spectra, weights = weights )

title: 18 Weighted Least Squares Unmixing
text: large experiments, computing weights single representative file reusing across samples two advantages: Consistency – unmixing matrix identical every file, comparisons files confounded per-file weight variation. Speed – unmix.fcs() skips weight-computation read weights provided, saving one FCS read per file. practical strategy choosing representative file: Concatenated file. Use concatenateFCS() combine samples one file, compute weights . single well-stained sample. know one file representative full experiment (e.g., fully stained PBMC sample), use . Avoid files missing markers unusually low cell counts, produce atypical per-channel means therefore atypical weights. Saved weights can also reloaded later R session:
code: # concatenate a random subset of all sample files concatenateFCS(   fcs.dir    = list.files(path = "./samples", pattern = ".fcs", full.names = TRUE),   output.name = "My merged.fcs"   output.dir =  "./concatenated_fcs" )  concat.file <- "./concatenated_fcs/My merged.fcs"  weights <- calculate.weights(   fcs.file          = concat.file,   spectral.channels = flow.control$spectral.channel,   save              = TRUE,   output.dir        = "./table_spectra" )  # now unmix all files with those weights unmix.folder(   sample.dir   = "./samples",   spectra      = spectra,   asp          = asp,   flow.control = flow.control,   method       = "WLS",   weights      = weights ) w <- utils::read.csv( "./table_spectra/weights.csv", row.names = 1 ) weights <- setNames( w[, 1], rownames( w ) )

<- "./Unmixed/Set1/Stained/D5 Lung_Set1.fcs"  asp.lung <- flowCore::exprs(   flowCore::read.FCS(autospectral.unmixed.lung,                      truncate_max_range = FALSE)   ) sf.lung <- flowCore::exprs(   flowCore::read.FCS(spectroflo.unmixed.lung,                      truncate_max_range = FALSE)   )  create.biplot(sf.lung, "BUV395-A", "BV421-A", asp, title = "SpectroFlo") create.biplot(asp.lung, "BUV395", "BV421", asp, title = "AutoSpectral")

title: AutoSpectral: Tools for Unmixing Spectral Flow Cytometry Data — AutoSpectral
text: maximize success AutoSpectral, workflow organized following logical step-wise processes: create.control.file: Generate .csv file contains descriptive information single stained controls Control File (see article) define.flow.control: uses result create.control.file convert FCS files associated metadata optimized data structure downstream workflows Flow Control (see article) clean.controls: removes autofluorescent events cell-based controls via PCA-based gating, selects top-expressing positive events, matches universal negatives scatter, downsamples speed get.fluorophore.spectra: extracts normalized [0, 1] fluorophore reference spectra cleaned controls performs cosine-similarity QC spectral reference library (Optional) get.af.spectra: extracts autofluorescence reference spectra use AutoSpectral unmixing method (Optional) get.spectral.variants: computes per-fluorophore spectral variants per-cell fluorophore optimization AutoSpectral unmixing unmix.fcs / unmix.folder: unmixes experiment FCS files using extracted spectra. Supported methods "OLS", "WLS", "Poisson", "AutoSpectral"

what: Assign AF Spectrum By Joint Covariance-Weighted Error — assign.af.joint.cov
title: Assign AF Spectrum By Joint Covariance-Weighted Error — assign.af.joint.cov
text: Assigns cell best-fitting autofluorescence spectral variant using joint scoring criterion multiplies two proportional error terms: covariance-weighted fluorophore error raw-space residual error. covariance AF spectra library propagated fluorophore space via unmixing matrix derive per-channel error weights, giving channels AF variation matters proportionally larger influence assignment decision. Multiplying two terms rewards variants achieve large improvements either axis, without requiring explicit mixing parameter. principled assign.af.fluorophores (plain L1) assign.af.residuals (simple residual dot-product) AF library contains spectrally diverse variants, covariance weights naturally downweight channels AF variants look similar upweight channels diverge.

what: Assign AF Spectrum By Scatter-Matched Reference Averaging — assign.af.scatter.match
title: Assign AF Spectrum By Scatter-Matched Reference Averaging — assign.af.scatter.match
text: experimental AF assignment approach benchmarking existing covariance/residual methods (assign.af.fluorophores, assign.af.residuals, assign.af.joint.cov). cell test.data, k nearest neighbours ref.data found Euclidean distance scatter space (using FNN::knnx.index). spectral channels averaged produce cell-specific reference spectrum. cosine similarity test cell's spectral signature scatter-matched reference average returned alongside best-fitting AF variant index, allowing direct comparison existing assignment approaches. rationale: cells identical scatter profiles (size, granularity) near-identical autofluorescence. scatter-matched average better proxy true AF library-based assignment, returned cosine similarities systematically higher obtained covariance/residual pipeline.

title: Assign AF Spectrum By Scatter-Matched Reference Averaging — assign.af.scatter.match
text: test.data Numeric matrix. Expression data test unstained FCS file. Cells rows, channels columns. Must contain scatter.param channels spectral detector channels. Can also path FCS file (character scalar), case file read readFCS(). ref.data Numeric matrix. Expression data reference unstained FCS file. column layout test.data. Can also FCS file path. scatter.param Character vector length >= 1. Column names identify scatter channels use kNN matching (e.g. c("FSC-", "SSC-")). excluded spectral similarity calculation. spectra Numeric matrix fluorophore spectra (fluorophores x detectors). Row names must fluorophore names; column names must match detector channels FCS file. k Integer. Number nearest reference neighbours average. Default 5. Larger values stabilise reference estimate may blur genuine AF heterogeneity. af.spectra Optional numeric matrix. Spectral signatures AF variants, normalised [0, 1], variants rows detectors columns. supplied, function also assigns test cell closest AF variant (cosine similarity scatter-matched average) returns index. Omit skip variant assignment. scale.scatter Logical. Whether z-score-standardise scatter channels computing kNN distances (recommended FSC SSC different dynamic ranges). what: Calculate Optimization Necessity Scores for Spectral Variants — calculate.optimize.necessity
title: Calculate Optimization Necessity Scores for Spectral Variants — calculate.optimize.necessity
text: Determines, fluorophore computed spectral variants, whether per-cell spectral optimisation likely produce meaningful improvement unmixing quality. Fluorophores whose spectral variation overlap detector channels occupied panel members improve unmixing channels regardless many variants tested, optimising wastes computation without benefit. score computed geometric space used C++ joint pipeline (unmix_autospectral_joint_cpp): fluorophore fl, pseudoinverse U_nof remaining fluorophores' spectra computed. matrix used inside FluorPrecomp build pc.w_leakage. empirical covariance fl's delta spectra (variant spread) propagated U_nof: leakage_cov = U_nof %*% delta_cov %*% t(U_nof). diagonal leakage_cov gives variance unmixing error induced fluorophore channel fl's spectral uncertainty. score fl sum standard deviations leakage: score(fl) = sum( sqrt( abs( diag( leakage_cov ) ) ) ). Scores normalised [0, 1] relative highest-scoring fluorophore panel. Optionally, representative stained sample provided (matrix per-fluorophore MFIs, typically median positive signal), scores additionally weighted mu[fl], fluorophore's brightness. -weights fluorophores geometrically capable cross-channel leakage always dim actual experiment unmixed. MFI weighting applied normalisation geometric score remains interpretable .

title: Calculate Optimization Necessity Scores for Spectral Variants — calculate.optimize.necessity
code: calculate.optimize.necessity(   spectra,   delta.list,   mu = NULL,   threshold = 0.01,   ridge = 1e-04,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate Optimization Necessity Scores for Spectral Variants — calculate.optimize.necessity
text: spectra Numeric matrix spectral signatures (fluorophores x detectors, values normalised 0-1). Must include "AF" row. delta.list Named list delta matrices, one per fluorophore. matrix (n_variants x n_detectors) contains element-wise difference variant spectrum base spectrum fluorophore. Produced get.spectral.variants() spectra.variants$delta.list. mu Named numeric vector per-fluorophore MFI values representative stained sample (one value per fluorophore spectra). Values positive; negative zero values clamped zero. Supply NULL (default) use geometric score alone without brightness weighting. threshold Numeric scalar [0, 1], default 0.01. Normalised score optimisation considered unnecessary fluorophore excluded optimize.recommended output. value 0.01 means fluorophores scoring 1% top-scoring fluorophore skipped. ridge Numeric scalar, default 1e-4. Ridge regularisation added diagonal delta_cov propagation, matching treatment inside C++ precomputation. Prevents degenerate covariance estimates fluorophore variants. verbose Logical, default TRUE. TRUE, prints table normalised scores resulting recommendation fluorophore.

title: Calculate Optimization Necessity Scores for Spectral Variants — calculate.optimize.necessity
text: named list three elements: scores.raw Named numeric vector raw (unnormalised) leakage propagation scores, one per fluorophore delta.list. scores.norm Named numeric vector scores normalised [0, 1]. optimize.recommended Named logical vector: TRUE fluorophore's normalised score meets exceeds threshold, FALSE otherwise. Fluorophores absent delta.list included.

what: Calculate Secondary Stain Index and Spillover — calculate.ssi
title: Calculate Secondary Stain Index and Spillover — calculate.ssi
text: Calculates Secondary Stain Index (SSI) secondary spillover every fluorophore channel unmixed dataset, given single-stained control matched unstained control. SSI defined : $$SSI = \frac{median(pos) - median(neg)}{2 \times rSD(neg)}$$ rSD robust standard deviation (median absolute deviation, MAD). Secondary spillover defined : $$Spillover = \frac{\Delta MFI_{channel}}{\Delta MFI_{\text{-}channel}}$$ Positive events identified single-stained cells whose intensity -target channel exceeds 99th percentile unstained control. 500 brightest positive events 500 randomly sampled unstained events used robustness speed.

title: Define Flow Control — define.flow.control
text: complex function designed convert single-stained control FCS files metadata control.def.file data structure AutoSpectral. reads metadata file, locates corresponding FCS files, determines gates needed based variables beads cells, large.gate, viability.gate, creates gates combination. imports gates data FCS files assigns various factors track data. Parallel processing parallel=TRUE likely speed run considerably Mac Linux systems supporting forking, likely help much Windows unless >10 cores available.

title: Define Flow Control — define.flow.control
code: define.flow.control(   control.dir,   control.def.file,   asp,   gate = TRUE,   gating.system = c("density", "landmarks"),   gate.list = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   threads = NULL,   color.palette = NULL )

title: Define Flow Control — define.flow.control
text: control.dir File path single-stained control FCS files. control.def.file CSV file defining single-color control file names, fluorophores represent, marker names, peak channels, gating requirements. asp AutoSpectral parameter list defined using get.autospectral.param. gate Logical, default TRUE, case, automated gating performed. FALSE, FCS files imported without automatically generated gates applied. , data files used. intended allow user pre-gate files commercial software. gating.system Character string selecting automated gating system employ defining initial scatter gates identifying cells FCS files. Options landmarks density. density option uses original gating system AutoSpill, picking cell populations based dense regions FSC/SSC. default landmarks system picks cell regions using brightest events peak channel single-stained control(s). approach generally robust. good way use utilize landmarks combination specifying single-stained control files used defining gates. example, abundant, bright cell markers CD4 reliably identify lymphocyte region, CD14 identify monocyte region, etc. instructions, see help pages GitHub. gate.list Optional named list gates. use , pre-define gates using define.gate.landmarks() /define.gate.density(), ensure names gates correspond names control.def.file, ensure gate.name column filled control.def.file. Default NULL revert creating new gates. parallel Logical, default FALSE, case parallel processing used. Parallel processing almost never faster now. verbose Logical, default TRUE. Set FALSE suppress messages. threads Numeric, number threads use parallel processing. Default NULL revert asp$worker.process.n parallel=TRUE. color.palette Optional character string defining viridis color palette used fluorophore traces. Use rainbow similar FlowJo SpectroFlo. options viridis color options: magma, inferno, plasma, viridis, cividis, rocket, mako turbo.

title: Define Gate by Density — define.gate.density
text: Performs gating scatter parameters based density search. gating proceeds three steps: Defines bounds data trimming Defines region around target maximum found within bounds Defines gate around target maximum, within region method uses numerical search maxima estimated densities Voronoi tessellations improve density estimation around maxima.

title: Define Gate by Density — define.gate.density
code: define.gate.density(   control.file,   control.dir,   asp,   gate.name,   gating.params = NULL,   n.cells = NULL,   grid.n = NULL,   bandwidth.factor = NULL,   target.pop = NULL,   neighbors = NULL,   fsc.channel = NULL,   ssc.channel = NULL,   fsc.lims = NULL,   ssc.lims = NULL,   fsc.search.min = NULL,   fsc.search.max = NULL,   ssc.search.min = NULL,   ssc.search.max = NULL,   output.dir = "./figure_gate",   filename = "density_gate_definition_",   color.palette = "plasma",   boundary.color = "black",   points.to.plot = 1e+05,   width = 5,   height = 5,   verbose = TRUE,   control.table = NULL )

title: Define Gate by Density — define.gate.density
text: control.file File path name CSV file defining single- color control file names, fluorophores represent, marker names, peak channels, gating requirements. control.dir File path single-stained control FCS files. asp AutoSpectral parameter list defined using get.autospectral.param. gate.name Character, name gate. Useful distinguishing gates multiple types. Must match one string (name) gate.name column control.file. gating.params Previously saved gating parameters. Load .rds file using readRDS pass result wish replicate previous run. essentially just updated version asp containing modifications based information passed arguments define.gate.landmarks. n.cells number cells use defining gate boundary. many cells selected specified FCS files. default, NULL, revert asp$gate.downsample.n. Around 1e5 recommended. grid.n Numeric, default 100. binning grid kernel density estimation. n.cells low, may wish lower number compress search space. bandwidth.factor Numeric, default NULL revert asp$gate.bound.density.bw.factor (usually 1). multiplier bandwidth kernel density estimation. Larger numbers smooth density, reducing discrimination peaks density (live dead cells). target.pop Numeric 0-n, default NULL revert asp$gate.bound.density.max.target (usually 1). output plots, numbers correspond density peaks. Whichever number set select population 1 less number target defining gate region. neighbors Number neighboring events consider finding local density maxima kernel density estimation. Default NULL revert asp$gate.bound.density.neigh.size, usually 3. fsc.channel Channel use Forward Scatter. Default NULL use asp$default.scatter.parameter[1], appropriate machine. ssc.channel Channel use Side Scatter. Default NULL use asp$default.scatter.parameter[2], appropriate machine. machines multiple side scatter measurements, can change . fsc.lims Numeric vector. Limits searching plotting FSC. default NULL uses cc(asp$scatter.data.min.x, asp$scatter.data.max.x). ssc.lims Numeric vector. Limits searching plotting SSC. default NULL uses cc(asp$scatter.data.min.y, asp$scatter.data.max.y). fsc.search.min minimum bound search space dense event populations defining gate x-axis. default NULL reverts asp$default.gate.param$region.factor.x.low, usually 0.05 5% FSC axis. fsc.search.max maximum bound search space dense event populations defining gate x-axis. default NULL reverts asp$default.gate.param$region.factor.x.high, usually 0.8 80% FSC axis. ssc.search.min minimum bound search space dense event populations defining gate y-axis. default NULL reverts asp$default.gate.param$region.factor.y.low, usually 0.05 5% SSC axis. ssc.search.max maximum bound search space dense event populations defining gate y-axis. default NULL reverts asp$default.gate.param$region.factor.y.high, usually 0.8 80% SSC axis.#' output.dir File path want save results. Default ./figure_gate. filename Character, name output files. Default gate_definition. color.palette Optional character string defining viridis color palette used fluorophore traces. Default plasma. Use rainbow similar FlowJo SpectroFlo. options viridis color options: magma, inferno, plasma, viridis, cividis, rocket, mako turbo. boundary.color Color gate boundary line plot. Default black. points..plot Numeric, default 1e5. Maximum number points show plot. points take longer, really even close number defining gate landmarks. width Numeric, default 5. Width saved plot. height Numeric, default 5. Height saved plot. verbose Logical, default TRUE. Set FALSE suppress messages. control.table Dataframe table control file, read via define.flow.control() cleaned function.

text: function performs quality control flow cytometry data using PeacoQC method. transforms data, removes margins, identifies good cells. function also optionally plots results saves cleaned data.

title: Perform Quality Control on Flow Cytometry Data using PeacoQC — do.peacoQC
code: do.peacoQC(   dirty.expr,   sample.name,   spectral.channel,   biexp.transform,   transform.inv,   output.dir,   time.param,   all.channels,   method = "MAD",   figures = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Perform Quality Control on Flow Cytometry Data using PeacoQC — do.peacoQC
text: dirty.expr matrix containing raw expression data, pre-cleaning. sample.name name sample. spectral.channel spectral channels used. biexp.transform biexponential transformation function. transform.inv inverse transformation function. output.dir directory save output files. time.param time channel parameter. .channels vector channels included final cleaned data. method PeacoQC method use. Inherited PeacoQC. Options , MAD . Default MAD. figures Logical. Controls whether PeacoQC plots created. Default TRUE. verbose Logical, default TRUE. Set FALSE suppress messages.

title: Perform Quality Control on Flow Cytometry Data using PeacoQC — do.peacoQC
text: matrix cleaned expression data.

title: Gate Matching Scatter — gate.scatter.match
text: Calculates density input scatter coordinates, cuts defined density percentile calculates convex hull boundary around points within dense region. Permits selection events similar forward side scatter profiles paired sample, e.g., unstained sample.

code: gate.scatter.match(scatter.data, percentile = 0.5, bw.factor = 1, grid.n = 10)

title: Gate Matching Scatter — gate.scatter.match
text: scatter.data Matrix forward side scatter data percentile Numeric 0-1. Percentile density use defining contour gate boundary. Default 0.5. bw.factor Numeric, scalar bandwidth multiplier. Larger numbers smooth density, expanding gate outwards. Default 1. grid.n Numeric, size grid defining kernel density estimate. Default 10.

title: Get Autofluorescence Spectra — get.af.spectra
text: Extracts autofluorescence spectra unstained sample. Intended use unmix.autospectral. Uses FlowSOM (EmbedSOM) clustering rapid identification cells similar AF profiles. Optionally deduplicates resulting spectra cosine similarity (deduplicate = TRUE, default) remove near-identical profiles cause spurious -correction near-zero events fully stained samples. refine = TRUE, second round targeted modulation performed cells remain far zero first-pass correction; modulated spectra screened redundancy base library appended.

title: Get Autofluorescence Spectra — get.af.spectra
code: get.af.spectra(   unstained.sample,   asp,   spectra,   som.dim = 10,   figures = TRUE,   save = TRUE,   plot.dir = NULL,   table.dir = NULL,   title = "Autofluorescence spectra",   verbose = TRUE,   deduplicate = FALSE,   duplication.threshold = 0.99,   refine = TRUE,   problem.quantile = 0.99,   remove.contaminants = TRUE,   contaminant.threshold = 0.99,   parallel = TRUE,   threads = if (parallel) 0 else 1,   heatmap.color.palette = "viridis",   spectral.trace.color.palette = NULL,   af.fill.color = "red",   af.line.color = "black" )

title: Get Autofluorescence Spectra — get.af.spectra
text: unstained.sample Path file name unstained sample FCS file. sample type processing (protocol) method match fully stained samples AF applied, ideally. asp AutoSpectral parameter list. Prepare using get.autospectral.param. spectra Spectral signatures fluorophores, normalized 0 1, fluorophores rows detectors columns. som.dim Number x y dimensions SOM. Default 10. figures Logical, whether plot spectral traces heatmap AF signatures. Default TRUE. save Logical, whether save CSV file AF signatures. Default TRUE. plot.dir Directory (folder) plots saved. Default NULL, inherits asp$figure.af.dir. table.dir Directory (folder) spectra csv file saved. Default NULL, inherits asp$table.af.dir. title Title output spectral plots csv file. Default "Autofluorescence spectra". verbose Logical, controls messaging. Default TRUE. deduplicate Logical, default FALSE. Whether deduplicate AF spectra cosine similarity base clustering stage refinement stage. Deduplication removes near-identical spectral profiles can cause overzealous matching near-zero events fully stained samples, reducing apparent "squishing". Deduplication slightly less accurate. Set TRUE us . duplication.threshold Numeric, default 0.99. cosine similarity threshold used deduplication. spectrum dropped cosine similarity already-retained spectrum meets exceeds value. used deduplicate = TRUE. refine Logical, default FALSE. Controls whether perform second round autofluorescence measurement "problem cells": highest residual fluorophore signal first-pass per-cell AF extraction, defined problem.quantile. FALSE, behavior identical versions AutoSpectral prior 1.0.0. working samples containing complex autofluorescence, e.g. tissues tumors, using refine = TRUE improve autofluorescence extraction cost increase unmixing time. problem.quantile Numeric, default 0.99. quantile determining cells "problematic" first-pass per-cell AF extraction. Cells quantile respect L2 norm unmixed fluorophore channels (.e. still furthest zero) selected second-round modulation. value 0.99 means top 1% cells. remove.contaminants Logical, default TRUE. QC check performed exclude autofluorescence spectrum nearly identical fluorophore signature spectra. guards low-level contamination unstained sample single-stained controls. contaminant.threshold Numeric, default 0.99. remove.contaminants = TRUE, events unstained sample whose cosine similarity fluorophore spectrum spectra meets exceeds value removed SOM construction. per-event filter sensitive post-SOM centroid check contaminating events unlikely dominate entire SOM node. Lower values aggressive; practical range roughly 0.98–0.999. parallel Logical, default TRUE, enables parallel processing per-cell AF identification. Used refine = TRUE. threads Numeric, defaults single thread sequential processing (parallel = FALSE) available cores parallel = TRUE. Used refine = TRUE. heatmap.color.palette Optional character string defining viridis color palette fluorophore heatmap. Default "viridis". Options: "magma", "inferno", "plasma", "viridis", "cividis", "rocket", "mako", "turbo". spectral.trace.color.palette Optional character string defining color palette AF traces. Default NULL (default R Brewer colors). Options: heatmap.color.palette. af.fill.color Color shaded region indicating range autofluorescence variation variant plot. Default "red". af.line.color Color median autofluorescence line variant plot. Default "black".

title: Get Autofluorescence Spectra — get.af.spectra
text: matrix autofluorescence spectra (spectra rows, detectors columns). Row 1 population mean base spectra; subsequent rows deduplicated base spectra , refine = TRUE, modulated spectra problem cells.

title: Get Fluorophore Variants — get.fluor.variants
text: Assesses variation spectral signature single-stained flow cytometry control sample using SOM clustering scatter-matched, background-corrected positive events. Autofluorescence characterised situ paired universal-negative file specified control table (lower 25\ available). AF mean vector unit-normalised projected event identify empirical peak detector, mirroring approach get.spectra.automated. positive events raw threshold (empirical) peak channel selected, n.cells (randomly downsampled present). selected event, \(k\) scatter-space nearest neighbours found unstained pool spectral values averaged form per-event background estimate, subtracted. SOM clustering resulting background-corrected matrix recovers population-level distribution spectral shapes. cosine-similarity QC step retains SOM centroids sufficiently similar reference spectrum, followed -peak smoothing.

title: Get Fluorophore Variants — get.fluor.variants
code: get.fluor.variants(   fluor,   file.name,   control.dir,   asp,   spectra,   figures,   output.dir,   verbose,   spectral.channel,   scatter.channel,   universal.negative,   control.type,   raw.thresholds,   unmixed.thresholds,   flow.channel,   af.pcs,   n.cells = 10000L,   som.dim = 10L,   k.neighbors = 3L,   sim.threshold = 0.985,   variant.fill.color = "red",   variant.fill.alpha = 0.7,   median.line.color = "black",   median.linewidth = 1 )

title: Get Fluorophore Variants — get.fluor.variants
text: fluor Character. Name fluorophore. file.name Named character vector control FCS filenames, named fluorophore. control.dir Character. Directory containing control FCS files. asp AutoSpectral parameter list get.autospectral.param(). spectra Numeric matrix. Reference spectra; fluorophores rows, detectors columns. figures Logical. Whether save spectral-variant plot. Default TRUE. output.dir Character. Directory figures. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default TRUE. spectral.channel Character vector spectral detector channel names. scatter.channel Character vector scatter parameter names (e.g. "FSC-", "SSC-") used KNN scatter matching unstained pool. universal.negative Named character vector mapping fluorophore names paired unstained FCS filename, "FALSE" / NA none available. control.type Character, either "beads" "cells". Determines type control sample used subsequent processing steps. raw.thresholds Named numeric vector per-channel positivity thresholds (typically 99.5th percentile unstained sample). unmixed.thresholds named vector numerical values corresponding threshold positivity unmixed channel. Determined 99.5th percentile unstained sample, typically single-cell AF unmixing. flow.channel Named character vector expected peak raw channels, one per fluorophore. af.pcs Named list autofluorescence-defining principal component matrices, one per unique unstained FCS file. Names FCS filenames matching entries universal.negative. n.cells Integer, default 10000. Maximum number positive events used SOM clustering. Files events threshold randomly downsampled number. som.dim Integer, default 10. Side length square SOM grid. Produces som.dim^2 candidate variant spectra cosine QC. k.neighbors Integer, default 3. Number scatter-space nearest neighbours unstained pool used form per-event background estimate. sim.threshold Numeric, default 0.985. Minimum cosine similarity SOM centroid reference spectrum centroid retained variant. variant.fill.color Color shaded ribbon variant plot. Default "red". variant.fill.alpha Alpha variant.fill.color. Default 0.7. median.line.color Color reference-spectrum line. Default "black". median.linewidth Width reference-spectrum line. Default 1.

title: Get Fluorophore Variants — get.fluor.variants
text: numeric matrix; variants rows, detectors columns, values normalised \([0, 1]\). centroids survive cosine QC single reference spectrum returned (one row).

what: Get Fluorophore Spectra - Automated Workflow — get.spectra.automated
title: Get Fluorophore Spectra - Automated Workflow — get.spectra.automated
text: Single-function replacement three-step define.flow.control -> clean.controls -> get.fluorophore.spectra pipeline. Extracts normalised [0, 1] fluorophore reference spectra directory single-stained control FCS files without requiring scatter gating interactive input. algorithm based spectracle approach, adapted use AutoSpectral's internal FCS reader, matrix-based data layout, control-file metadata already required rest AutoSpectral pipeline: Reads single-stained controls readFCS(). Removes saturating events. #' 3. fluorophore, determines AF reference paired universal-negative file specified universal.negative column control table, - column empty - uses internal-negative mode. External-negative: projection-based AF orthogonalisation identify empirical peak detector; cosine-similarity filter select least AF-like events; KNN scatter-matched AF subtraction; column median summary. Internal-negative: top-100 events expected peak channel minus mean bottom-10%; row mean summary; cosine filter kNN step. Applies QC: normalised signal cosine similarity spectral reference library cosine.threshold, spectrum refined using legacy gating cleaning approach. Returns spectra matrix AutoSpectral format (fluorophores x detectors). legacy workflow (define.flow.control + clean.controls + get.fluorophore.spectra) remains available unchanged.

title: Get Fluorophore Spectra - Automated Workflow — get.spectra.automated
code: get.spectra.automated(   control.dir,   control.def.file,   asp,   n.candidates = 1000L,   n.spectral = 200L,   k.neighbors = 2L,   singlet.quantiles = c(0.85, 0.975),   cosine.threshold = 0.9,   peak.signal.threshold = 0.5,   legacy.refinement = TRUE,   top.expressing.override = NULL,   return.af = FALSE,   figures = TRUE,   plot.cosine.filter = TRUE,   plot.scatter.match = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Get Fluorophore Spectra - Automated Workflow — get.spectra.automated
text: control.dir Character. Path directory containing single-stained control FCS files. control.def.file Character. Path (filename ) control definition CSV. Must already exist pass check.control.file(). asp AutoSpectral parameter list get.autospectral.param(). n.candidates Integer, default 1000. Number top-expressing candidate events selected per fluorophore cosine-similarity filtering. Ignored internal-negative mode, top 5%% events peak channel used directly. n.spectral Integer, default 200. Number spectral events retained filtering low AF cosine similarity. k.neighbors Integer, default 2. Number nearest neighbours scatter space used per-event AF subtraction. singlet.quantiles Numeric, default c( 0.85, 0.975 ). Range quantiles use doublet discrimination. cosine.threshold Numeric, default 0.9. Minimum cosine similarity spectral reference library accept automated spectrum; values threshold trigger legacy pipeline refinement. peak.signal.threshold Numeric, default 0.5. Minimum normalised signal expected peak detector. Used informational QC. legacy.refinement Logical, default TRUE. Whether run legacy pipeline controls signature match reference well. top.expressing.override Named numeric vector NULL (default). Override event count specific samples. Names match FCS filename (without extension) fluorophore name control file. return.af Logical, default FALSE. Adds single mean autofluorescence spectrum output allow unmixing OLS WLS using autofluorescence extraction. Requires naming unstained cell control sample AF fluorophore control file. figures Logical, default TRUE. Produce spectral trace, heatmap, cosine-similarity, reference-library QC plots. plot.cosine.filter Logical, default TRUE. figures = TRUE, produce multi-panel PDF showing per-fluorophore cosine-similarity filter traces (binned cosine similarity AF, coloured least AF-like). Set FALSE skip. plot.scatter.match Logical, default TRUE. figures = TRUE, produce multi-panel PDF showing KNN scatter-matched AF subtraction fluorophore (unstained background, selected spectral events, matched AF events). Set FALSE skip. verbose Logical, default TRUE. Print progress messages.

title: Get Fluorophore Spectra - Automated Workflow — get.spectra.automated
text: numeric matrix fluorophores rows spectral detector channels columns, values normalised [0, 1] (L-infinity norm, peak = 1). Compatible downstream AutoSpectral functions.

title: Get Spectral Variations for Fluorophores — get.spectral.variants
text: control.dir Character. Path single-stained control FCS files. control.def.file Character. Path control definition CSV. Must pass check.control.file(). asp AutoSpectral parameter list get.autospectral.param(). spectra Numeric matrix. Reference spectra; fluorophores rows, detectors columns. figures Logical, default TRUE. Whether save variant-spectrum plots. output.dir Character NULL. Directory figures .rds output file. Defaults asp$variant.dir. parallel Logical, default FALSE. Enable parallel processing across fluorophores. verbose Logical, default TRUE. Set FALSE suppress messages. threads Numeric NULL. Number parallel workers. Defaults asp$worker.process.n. n.cells Integer, default 10000. Maximum positive events per fluorophore used SOM clustering. Files events threshold randomly downsampled. Passed get.fluor.variants. som.dim Integer, default 10. Side length square SOM grid; som.dim^2 candidate variants per fluorophore cosine QC. Passed get.fluor.variants. k.neighbors Integer, default 3. Number scatter-space nearest neighbours unstained pool used estimate per-event background. Passed get.fluor.variants. sim.threshold Numeric, default 0.99. Minimum cosine similarity reference spectrum SOM centroid retained variant. Passed get.fluor.variants. variant.fill.color Color shaded ribbon variant plots. Default "red". variant.fill.alpha Alpha variant.fill.color. Default 0.7. median.line.color Color reference-spectrum line. Default "black". median.linewidth Width reference-spectrum line. Default 1. stained.sample Optional file path representative stained FCS file. supplied, read unmixed obtain per-fluorophore median positive signal (MFI), weights optimization necessity scores fluorophore brightness. Pass NULL (default) use purely geometric scores. optimize.necessity.threshold Numeric [0, 1], default 0.01. Passed calculate.optimize.necessity(). Fluorophores whose normalised leakage score falls value flagged requiring per-cell spectral optimisation. result stored $optimize.recommended returned list used automatically unmix.autospectral.rcpp() skip unnecessary optimisation passes. ... Ignored. Catches warns previously used deprecated arguments: af.spectra, refine, problem.quantile, pos.quantile.

what: Simulate Spectral Flow Cytometry Data — sim.flow.data
title: Simulate Spectral Flow Cytometry Data — sim.flow.data
text: Generates synthetic spectral flow cytometry data development validation AutoSpectral pipelines. Cells assigned random fluorophore expression levels drawn three discrete expression layers (high, medium, low), mixed detector space via provided spectra (optionally spectral variants), corrupted configurable noise sources: binomial spillover sampling, Poisson shot noise, detector readout noise. Autofluorescence variation supported via provided af.spectra matrix. Ground-truth abundances, per-cell variant assignments, per-cell AF spectra, synthetic scatter returned alongside raw detector-space matrix simple OLS unmixed result (AF) immediate inspection.

title: Simulate Spectral Flow Cytometry Data — sim.flow.data
code: sim.flow.data(   spectra,   asp,   n.cells = 10000L,   complexity = 1/3,   layer.centroids = c(0.5, 0.05, 0.005),   layer.cv = 0.2,   af.spectra = NULL,   af.scale.range = c(0.001, 0.05),   af.scatter.slope = 0.1,   variants = NULL,   scatter.data = NULL,   fsc.mean.log = log(2e+05),   fsc.sd.log = 0.4,   ssc.mean.log = log(80000),   ssc.sd.log = 0.5,   fsc.ssc.cor = 0.6,   shot.noise = TRUE,   counts.per.unit = 0.5,   spillover.noise = TRUE,   detector.noise = TRUE,   af.variation = TRUE,   spectral.variation = TRUE,   seed = 42L )

title: Simulate Spectral Flow Cytometry Data — sim.flow.data
text: spectra Numeric matrix fluorophore spectral signatures, L-infinity normalised, fluorophores rows detectors columns. Required. Defines panel detector layout. asp AutoSpectral parameter list get.autospectral.param. Used resolve expr.data.max cytometer-specific detector noise presets. n.cells Integer. Number synthetic cells generate. Default 10000. complexity Numeric (0, 1]. Fraction fluorophores "" (non-zero) per cell, drawn without replacement. Default 1/3. layer.centroids Numeric vector length 3, giving high, medium, low expression centroids fractions expr.data.max. Default c(0.5, 0.05, 0.005). layer.cv Numeric scalar. Coefficient variation (log scale) expression within layer. Default 0.2. af.spectra Numeric matrix autofluorescence spectra, L-infinity normalised, AF components rows detectors columns. returned get.af.spectra. NULL (default), AF added. af.scale.range Numeric vector length 2. Range uniform distribution per-cell AF abundance drawn, fraction expr.data.max. Default c(0.001, 0.05). af.scatter.slope Numeric scalar. Controls much SSC drives AF abundance. AF abundance multiplied 1 + af.scatter.slope * (ssc - median(ssc)) / mad(ssc). Default 0.1. variants List spectral variant matrices returned get.spectral.variants get.fluor.variants. element named fluorophore contains matrix variant spectra (variants rows, detectors columns). NULL (default), base spectra rows used cells. scatter.data Optional numeric matrix real scatter data (cells rows, least two columns FSC SSC). provided, rows sampled replacement generate synthetic scatter, bypassing log-normal model. Default NULL. fsc.mean.log Numeric. Mean log-normal FSC distribution (natural-log scale). Default log(200000). fsc.sd.log Numeric. SD log-normal FSC distribution. Default 0.4. ssc.mean.log Numeric. Mean log-normal SSC distribution. Default log(80000). ssc.sd.log Numeric. SD log-normal SSC distribution. Default 0.5. fsc.ssc.cor Numeric (-1, 1). Pearson correlation log(FSC) log(SSC) synthetic scatter population. Default 0.6. shot.noise Logical. Whether apply Poisson shot noise. Default TRUE. counts.per.unit Numeric scalar shot.noise. Default 0.5. Works inversely; higher numbers decrease shot noise-driven spread. spillover.noise Logical. Whether apply binomial spillover sampling. Default TRUE. detector.noise Logical. Whether apply detector readout noise (Gaussian, cytometer-specific). Default TRUE. af.variation Logical. Whether include autofluorescence variation (requires af.spectra). Default TRUE. spectral.variation Logical. Whether sample per-cell spectral variants (requires variants). Default TRUE. seed Integer random seed reproducibility. Default 42.

title: Simulate Spectral Flow Cytometry Data — sim.flow.data
text: named list elements: raw Numeric matrix (cells x detectors) simulated detector-space data, including enabled noise sources. unmixed..af Numeric matrix (cells x fluorophores) OLS-unmixed data without AF, immediate inspection. truth Named list: abundances Matrix (cells x fluorophores+AF), true abundance per cell. AF column appended last column af.spectra provided. expression.layer Character matrix (cells x fluorophores), "high", "mid", "low", NA (). variant.index Integer matrix (cells x fluorophores), index variant spectrum used per cell per fluorophore. NA variants provided. af.row Integer vector (length cells), row af.spectra assigned cell. NA AF. af.abundance Numeric vector (length cells), true AF abundance per cell instrument units. 0 AF. scatter Numeric matrix (cells x 2, columns FSC SSC), synthetic resampled scatter values. params Named list simulation settings used, reproducibility.

text: ref.spectrum named numeric vector (single-row matrix) normalised spectral intensities reference spectrum. Names must correspond detector channel names. test.spectrum named numeric vector (single-row matrix) normalised spectral intensities test spectrum. Must names ref.spectrum. ref.label Character string used legend label reference spectrum. NULL (default), rownames() ref.spectrum used. test.label Character string used legend label test spectrum. text: unstained.fcs Character scalar. Path unstained FCS file used reference data set. spectra Numeric matrix fluorophore spectra (fluorophores x detectors). Row names must fluorophore names; column names must match detector channels FCS file. row named "AF" removed automatically processing. af.spectra Numeric matrix AF spectra (AF variants x detectors). first row treated mean AF spectrum used baseline comparison. Column names must match spectra. asp Aspect-ratio value passed create.biplot. functions Character vector AF-function names benchmark. name must resolve function current search path. Maximum number events read FCS file. random subsample size drawn file contains events. Set Inf use events. Default: 1000L. plot.dir Character scalar. Directory save biplot PDF. Created recursively exist. Default: "figure_af_accuracy". title Character scalar. Stem used name output PDF (file <plot.dir>/<title>_biplots.pdf). Default: "af_accuracy".

text: control.file File path name CSV file defining single- color control file names, fluorophores represent, marker names, peak channels, gating requirements. control.dir File path single-stained control FCS files. asp AutoSpectral parameter list defined using get.autospectral.param. gate.name Character, name gate. Useful distinguishing gates multiple types. Must match one string (name) gate.name column control.file. n.cells number cells use defining gate boundary. default c(100, 500, 2000) test values. many cells selected peak channel (brightest first) single- color controls. example, set 200 marked files CD3-PE.fcs CD19-FITC.fcs gate.define=TRUE control file, brightest 200 events YG1 channel CD3-PE file brightest 200 events B1 channel CD19-FITC file used define gate. percentiles Numeric 1 - 100, default c(30, 50, 70). percentile cutoffs test density scatter use defining gate. example, value 50 take 50% cells closest density peak, less. Smaller numbers define tighter gate. grid.n Numeric, default 100. binning grid kernel density estimation. n.cells low, may wish lower number compress search space. bandwidth.factor Numeric, default 1. multiplier bandwidth kernel density estimation. Larger numbers smooth density, reducing discrimination peaks density (live dead cells). fsc.channel Channel use Forward Scatter. Default NULL use asp$default.scatter.parameter[1], appropriate machine. ssc.channel Channel use Side Scatter. Default NULL use asp$default.scatter.parameter[2], appropriate machine. machines multiple side scatter measurements, can change . fsc.lims Numeric vector. Limits plotting FSC. default NULL uses cc(asp$scatter.data.min.x, asp$scatter.data.max.x). ssc.lims Numeric vector. Limits plotting SSC. default NULL uses cc(asp$scatter.data.min.y, asp$scatter.data.max.y). output.dir File path want save results. Default ./figure_gate_tuning. filename Character, name output files. Default gate_tuning. color.palette Optional character string defining viridis color palette used fluorophore traces. Default plasma. Use rainbow similar FlowJo SpectroFlo. options viridis color options: magma, inferno, plasma, viridis, cividis, rocket, mako turbo. boundary.color Color gate boundary line plot. Default darkgoldenrod1. points..plot Numeric, default 1e5. Maximum number points show plot. points take longer, really even close number defining gate landmarks. width Numeric, default 4. Width saved plot. height Numeric, default 4. Height saved plot.
